prompts: Brownian motion is the random movement of particles suspended in a fluid, resulting from their collision with fast-moving molecules in the fluid. This phenomenon was named after botanist Robert Brown, who observed pollen grains randomly moving in water under a microscope in 1827. Brownian motion exemplifies the unpredictability and chaotic dynamics of microscale systems, further contributing to the foundation of statistical mechanics and thermodynamics. Albert Einstein provided a theoretical explanation for this motion in 1905, validating the molecular nature of fluids and supporting the kinetic theory of heat through mathematical models.
